fixtures.description: 1 second of silence. The pipeline must produce no transcription. Plumbing fixture: proves the harness loads models, opens the WAV, runs the pipeline, and collects events without producing spurious output.

Pinned 2026-04-26. Real ~32s utterance ('I want you to undo the fix...') with a repeated phrase ('I want you' / 'why don't you do an investigation' / 'have a conversation') that historically gave the finalizer a tempting false anchor and dropped the first ~50 words, emitting only the trailing 'reproduce it, then let's have a conversation...'. Under the dual-stream pipeline the full transcript must still keep the prefix. Regression: any change that reintroduces a prefix-dropping path will fail the must_contain assertions.

Pinned 2026-04-26 (re-pinned from the original 400ms-gap variant when recovery_window_ms tightened from 500 to 250). Synthesized: turn-000008 ('For reference...') + 200 ms of zero-padded silence + turn-000009 ('Of note...'). The 200 ms gap fits inside the new 250 ms recovery window, so the pipeline correctly merges into ONE turn / ONE paste. Asserts the positive direction: recovery DOES fire on a gap inside the window.

Companion to recovery-bridges-200ms-gap. Same source clips with a 400 ms inserted gap. Under the current low in-speech VAD threshold, soft trailing speech keeps the turn active and the pipeline merges this into ONE turn. Catches future VAD/recovery changes that unexpectedly split this soft-continuation shape.
